assertionResults.reason: The only Red Team review bead found (sc-d6f) has no dependency links, so no review->batch blocking dependency exists and readiness gating cannot be enforced.

The Red Team review bead content is only 'Testing blocking review' with no numbered objections, no problem/impact/suggested-fix structure, and no comments, which violates the actionable-objections contract.
No Red Team post-mortem beads are present in the current beads data, so advisory non-blocking dependency behavior cannot be validated.

frictions.description: The local bd CLI version does not support some older list-output flags used in prior evidence conventions, so equivalent commands had to use current flags (--flat/--all) for reproducible output capture.
frictions.resolution: Switched to bd list --all --flat and continued validation with equivalent read-only checks.

blockers.description: No post-mortem bead artifacts were found for Red Team in the scbench beads workspace.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Searched with both 'post-mortem' and 'postmortem' terms across all statuses and reviewed full issue listings; no post-mortem artifacts were present.

summary: Validated 3 assertions via shell/bd checks. VAL-ROLES-005 and VAL-ROLES-006 failed due to missing dependency gate wiring and missing structured objections, and VAL-ROLES-007 is blocked because no post-mortem bead exists to inspect.
